title: A Noble Boke off Cookry
summary: The repository XML is a local copy/version of University of Michigan CME item CME00005. U-M's digital text encodes selected contents of Robina Napier's 1882 edition, whose main text transcribes the surviving culinary compilation in Holkham Hall MS 674. A complete public scan of the 1882 edition has been cached, but no public whole-manuscript facsimile of MS 674 was found as of 2026-07-11. Holkham accepts research and reproduction enquiries; a request should seek the complete codex and then map this work within it rather than ordering only presumed work leaves.
review_notes: The abstract culinary compilation, physical manuscript, 1882 edition, U-M encoding, and repository XML are separate entities with separate access and rights records.
The ignored source cache preserves whole publicly available source objects when possible; work_portion records identify the relevant pages or canvases within those whole objects.
Negative availability findings are date-bounded and do not prove that no private, preservation, or unindexed digitization exists.
Rights in medieval text, old editions, provider scans, and newly commissioned manuscript images must not be inherited from one another.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00005
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's conversion and publication workflow, supplied through the Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: IDG/@ID, BIBNO, and VID identify the file as CME00005.
The source submodule is a distinct repository and is not changed by this manifest.

entities.label: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse item CME00005
entities.description: A publicly accessible keyed and structurally marked-up digital text based on Napier's 1882 edition.

date_statements.label: repository XML revision
date_statements.notes: The XML TEMPHEAD records a structural review date, not necessarily the first online publication date.

notes: U-M's collection-level account says the 2016-2018 additions were manually keyed from public-domain editions and proofread; no item-level production log was found for CME00005.

entities.label: Napier 1882 edition

bibliographic.citation: A Noble Boke off Cookry ffor a Prynce Houssolde or Eny Other Estately Houssolde: Reprinted Verbatim from a Rare MS. in the Holkham Collection, ed. Mrs. Alexander Napier (London: Elliot Stock, 1882).
bibliographic.title: A Noble Boke off Cookry ffor a Prynce Houssolde or Eny Other Estately Houssolde

notes: The main manuscript transcription occupies printed pages 1-123; Napier's introduction, appendix, glossary, and errata are separate modern editorial matter.
The title-page claim 'reprinted verbatim' is an edition statement, not independent proof that every spelling, punctuation mark, expansion, or visual feature exactly reproduces the manuscript.

entities.label: Holkham Hall MS 674
alternate_labels: A Noble Boke off Cookry manuscript
entities.description: The sole identified manuscript witness of the culinary compilation printed by Napier. It is privately held at Holkham Hall by the Earl of Leicester and the Trustees of the Holkham Estate.

date_statements.label: Napier's internal-date argument
date_statements.value: written after the 1467 Neville feast, probably shortly afterwards
date_statements.notes: This relative date is Napier's inference from the menus and handwriting, preserved separately from the modern museum dating.

holding.notes: The current Fitzwilliam exhibition label credits the Earl of Leicester and the Trustees of the Holkham Estate; no item-level Holkham online catalog record was found.

physical_description.extent: About 80 leaves according to Napier's 1881 description
notes: Napier describes a small volume written neatly in black ink, with headings and ornamentation in red.
The manuscript had been rebound by 1881 and showed damp damage in places but was otherwise in excellent condition.
The first eleven leaves contain feast menus; the remaining leaves contain household-service fragments, a calendar, and recipes.
Napier's final 1882 edition reports 27 missing recipes between 'Zoupes chett' and 'Lesk lombard'. Exact modern foliation and collation were not found in an openly accessible current catalog.
No public whole-codex or selected-leaf manuscript images were found during the date-bounded search recorded below.

A reproduction request should identify MS 674 and ask for the complete codex: front and back covers or boards, spine, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, every recto and verso, and any inserted or detached material. Exact work foliation should be assigned only after those images or an authoritative foliation are available.

entities.label: Wellcome Collection digital facsimile of Napier's 1882 edition
entities.description: A complete 164-canvas digital surrogate of the printed edition, with public PDF and OCR renderings. It is a facsimile of the edition, not of Holkham Hall MS 674.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 3 page images, PDF, and OCR text
notes: The PDF includes the complete printed volume and a provider rights page; the IIIF presentation contains 164 physical canvases.
The medieval transcription maps to printed pages 1-123 and IIIF canvases 21-143.

entities.label: Napier's 1881 description of the Holkham cookery manuscript

bibliographic.citation: R. N. [Robina Napier], 'An Early Cookery Book,' The Antiquary 4 (September 1881): 96-98.
bibliographic.title: An Early Cookery Book

notes: The preliminary article says eight leaves and thirty recipes were missing; Napier's final 1882 edition instead specifies twenty-seven missing recipes, so the final count is preferred while preserving the earlier physical description.

entities.label: Holkham Library and Archives access and image-fee policies
entities.description: The current Hall page links these 2023 PDFs for researcher access and digital-image orders. They describe enquiry procedures, preservation discretion, current linked fee schedules, and restrictions on reproduction and publication.
notes: The archive-study fee schedule should not automatically be applied to manuscript-library consultation without confirmation from Holkham.
The linked image-fee schedule is dated 2023; request a current quote before relying on it.

agents.name: Holkham Hall, the Earl of Leicester and the Trustees of the Holkham Estate

subject.description: The complete local CME00005 XML artifact

object.description: University of Michigan CME item CME00005

scope.notes: Identifiers, bibliographic header, structure, and text match. The exact transfer date and any intermediate normalization are not documented.

object.description: Napier's modern title page and main medieval transcription, especially printed pages 1-123

scope.notes: The XML does not encode Napier's introduction, appendix, glossary, or errata as text, and it supplies only a FIGDESC for the banquet illustration.

object.description: The surviving medieval textual contents of MS 674; this does not mean that Napier reproduced bindings, blanks, physical damage, color, or every codicological feature

scope.notes: Napier calls the edition verbatim and prints the manuscript's closing trailer. Exact visual and diplomatic fidelity remains to be tested against the manuscript.

subject.description: All 164 IIIF canvases and the complete PDF rendering

object.description: The small Holkham cookery volume later printed by Napier

Pinned GitHub source and raw download
Public web item and full-text view
Wellcome item viewer, complete PDF, or IIIF Presentation manifest

local_copies.label: Complete Wellcome PDF of Napier's 1882 edition
notes: The cached file preserves the complete printed volume, including title matter, introduction, medieval transcription, appendix, glossary, errata, and a provider rights page.

work_portion.label: Main medieval transcription of A Noble Boke off Cookry
notes: Direct inspection confirms that canvas 21 opens printed page 1 and canvas 143 ends printed page 123 with the final recipe and manuscript trailer.

local_copies.label: Complete Wellcome IIIF Presentation manifest for Napier's 1882 edition
notes: The cached manifest describes all 164 canvases and preserves provider, rights, image-service, PDF-rendering, and OCR-rendering links; it does not itself contain the image pixels.

work_portion.label: Canvas range for the main medieval transcription

access.format: IIIF page images, PDF, and structured metadata
notes: This complete digital object reproduces the 1882 printed edition, not the medieval manuscript.

local_copies.label: Complete OCR text derivative of Napier's 1882 edition
notes: Machine-generated OCR of the complete scan; spelling, layout, and character recognition require checking against the page images.

work_portion.label: OCR corresponding to the main medieval transcription

access.access_method: Exact shelfmark and title searches across Holkham, the Fitzwilliam label, Cambridge Digital Library, Scriptorium, Biblissima, Bridgeman Images, Digital Bodleian, and the Bodleian/MARCO Holkham microfilm collection
notes: No public whole-codex facsimile or selected-leaf manuscript images were found. This does not establish that MS 674 has never been digitized or photographed privately.
Bodleian shelfmark MS. Film 674 reproduces a group of other Holkham manuscripts and must not be confused with Holkham Hall MS 674.

access.access_method: Email Holkham's Library and Manuscripts team to request a research appointment and confirm access conditions for MS 674
access.format: Onsite consultation by prior arrangement, subject to access and preservation conditions
notes: The currently linked PDF includes archive study fees, but the applicability of those fees to this manuscript-library item should be confirmed directly.

access.access_method: Email the Library and Manuscripts team for availability and a current quotation; request the complete physical codex first, then map the work within the delivered image sequence
access.format: Digital images by in-house low-resolution photography where available or full digitization through Cambridge University Library, subject to quotation and preservation constraints
notes: Specify MS 674 and request front cover or board, spine, back cover or board, pastedowns, flyleaves, all blanks, every leaf recto and verso, inserts, stubs, detached fragments, and a color target or scale if available.
Ask Holkham to preserve or supply its authoritative foliation and image sequence so the first eleven menu leaves and the remaining calendar and recipe material can be mapped after delivery.

The currently linked 2023 schedule lists in-house low-resolution rates of GBP 18 per image for 1-2 images, GBP 12 for 3-20, and GBP 6 for 21-40; full digitization through Cambridge is listed at GBP 18 for 1-2, GBP 12 for 3-20, GBP 6 for 21-99, and GBP 3.50 for 100 or more, plus a GBP 350 transfer/administration fee, excluding VAT. Confirm all rates and the image count before ordering.
Holkham states that preservation concerns may prevent photography and that it does not maintain an in-house high-resolution photographer.

access.access_method: Internet Archive viewer, complete issue PDF, or OCR

local_copies.label: Complete September 1881 issue of The Antiquary
notes: The cache preserves the complete 48-page issue, not merely the three-page article.

local_copies.label: Complete OCR text for the September 1881 issue of The Antiquary
notes: Machine-generated OCR of the complete issue; quoted wording was checked against the PDF page images.

work_portion.label: OCR corresponding to 'An Early Cookery Book'

access.format: Page images, PDF, and plain-text OCR

access.access_method: Direct policy PDF downloads linked from Holkham's Hall page

rights.component: The CME00005 digital text and materials presented by University of Michigan
rights.basis: The U-M item and collection pages state that the materials are in the public domain and apply the DPLA No Copyright-United States statement.
notes: No Copyright-United States is a rights statement, not an affirmative worldwide license.
The statement does not apply automatically to newly commissioned Holkham manuscript images.

rights.component: Upstream CME transcription and underlying 1882 source content represented in the local XML
rights.basis: The local artifact is supported as a version of U-M CME00005, which U-M marks No Copyright-United States; the immediate edition was published in 1882.
notes: This assessment does not override repository licenses for new project-authored code, metadata, formatting, or outputs.

rights.component: The 1882 edition, transcription, introduction, notes, appendix, and glossary
rights.basis: The edition was published in 1882, and U-M independently marks the CME item derived from it No Copyright-United States.

rights.component: The Wellcome/Leeds digital surrogate, including page images and PDF rendering
rights.basis: Wellcome marks the digital object with the Creative Commons Public Domain Mark and states that it can be used for any purpose without restriction.
notes: The Public Domain Mark is a rights label, not a license.
This provider assertion covers the Wellcome digital object and does not convert it into a manuscript facsimile.

rights.component: The anonymous medieval textual content of the manuscript
rights.basis: The manuscript is dated c. 1480-1500 and its underlying historical text is far outside any U.S. copyright term.
notes: This concerns the old textual content, not modern photographs, digital files, catalog prose, transcriptions, or access agreements.

rights.component: Any new digital photographs or scans supplied by Holkham or its imaging partner in response to a reproduction order
rights.jurisdiction: United Kingdom and the intended place of reuse
rights.basis: No item-specific images, delivery agreement, or reuse grant is currently recorded. Holkham's linked policy says copyright is retained, supplies images for noncommercial research or private study, and requires formal permission for reproduction or publication.
notes: Do not commit commissioned manuscript images to git unless Holkham gives written permission covering public redistribution and the repository's commercial print-on-demand context.

Preserve the written quotation, credit line, delivery terms, image creator, creation date, and permission response. If redistribution is refused, retain only the private ignored research cache and publish the access route and locators.
Public-domain status of the medieval text does not waive contractual or image-use conditions attached to supplied files.

editorial_practices.representation: The XML encodes the edition title page and the medieval transcription but omits the modern introduction, appendix, glossary, and errata as text
editorial_practices.description: CME00005 is not a full digital edition of every page in Napier's volume. Its historical-text content corresponds principally to printed pages 1-123.

editorial_practices.source_feature: The banquet woodcut or illustration on the edition's preliminary page
editorial_practices.representation: A FIGDESC reading 'men and women seated at banquet table' rather than embedded image pixels
editorial_practices.description: The XML preserves a textual description of the edition illustration, not a facsimile of it and not an image from MS 674.
editorial_practices.scope: Edition preliminary matter represented in CME00005

editorial_practices.source_feature: Manuscript headings and ornamentation in red ink
editorial_practices.representation: Black printed typography, headings, decorative initials, and later XML structural markup; exact feature-by-feature correspondence is unresolved
editorial_practices.description: Napier's 1881 description confirms red headings and ornament in the manuscript, while the monochrome printed edition and XML cannot recover their color or exact appearance.
editorial_practices.scope: Headings and ornament throughout Holkham Hall MS 674
notes: A color facsimile of the complete manuscript is required to map each red feature to Napier's typography and the XML encoding.

open_questions.question: Does a complete digital or preservation facsimile of Holkham Hall MS 674 exist outside the public sources searched, and what is the manuscript's authoritative modern foliation?
next_steps: Ask Holkham whether a complete existing surrogate can be supplied before commissioning new photography.
If new work is required, request the complete codex, including covers or boards, spine, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, every recto and verso, stubs, inserts, and detached material.

After receipt, inventory the full image sequence and record the exact folio or image range for the feast menus, service fragments, calendar, 253 extant recipe units, final trailer, and any lost-leaf gaps.
notes: The best current descriptive boundary is approximately eighty leaves, with feast menus on the first eleven; it is not a substitute for foliation.
No public copy found is not synonymous with not digitized.

open_questions.question: How exactly do Napier's spelling, punctuation, expansions, headings, and typography correspond to the manuscript's writing and red ornament?
next_steps: Collate Napier's complete printed transcription against a color whole-codex facsimile leaf by leaf.

Document manuscript abbreviation signs, supplied letters, punctuation, lineation, rubrication, decorative initials, corrections, and damaged or missing passages separately from Napier's representations.
Treat the title-page word 'verbatim' as Napier's edition statement until the collation is complete.

open_questions.question: What item-specific copyright, credit-line, contractual, and redistribution terms would Holkham apply to a complete MS 674 facsimile?
next_steps: Ask expressly whether the delivered images may be placed in a public git repository that supports nonprofit and for-profit print-on-demand publication.
Retain the written response and delivery terms, and record image creator, creation date, checksum, extent, technical metadata, and required credit line.
If public redistribution is not granted, keep the images only in the ignored research cache and expose the source citation, request route, and work locators rather than the files.

open_questions.question: What exact U-M release, transfer date, and transformation history produced the pinned local CME00005 XML blob?
next_steps: Inspect surviving CME repository history and U-M bulk-release documentation for an item-level production record.
Keep the supported copied-from relation distinct from an undocumented byte-for-byte identity claim.

evidence.citation: General Corpus / CME source XML, CME00005.xml.
evidence.summary: Identifies CME00005 and Napier's edition; encodes edition title matter, feast menus, household-service fragments, a calendar, 253 recipe divisions, page breaks, and the final manuscript trailer; records a 2016 structural review.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse, item CME00005.
evidence.locator: item information, table of contents, full text, subjects, and rights
evidence.summary: The official item identifies Napier's 1882 edition, provides the encoded text and recipe headings, and marks the materials public domain with No Copyright-United States.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse, 'About this collection'.
evidence.locator: collection history, 2016-2018 additions, production statement, and rights
evidence.summary: The collection account says approximately 150 texts added in 2016-2018 were manually keyed from public-domain editions and proofread, and applies No Copyright-United States at collection level.

evidence.citation: A Noble Boke off Cookry ffor a Prynce Houssolde or Eny Other Estately Houssolde: Reprinted Verbatim from a Rare MS. in the Holkham Collection, ed. Mrs. Alexander Napier (London: Elliot Stock, 1882).
evidence.locator: title page; introduction pp. v-xiii; main transcription pp. 1-123; note following p. 123; appendix, glossary, and errata pp. 124-136
evidence.summary: The immediate source edition identifies Napier, Holkham, the 1882 London publication, the post-1467 dating argument, the main manuscript transcription, and 27 recipes missing from the surviving sequence.

evidence.citation: Wellcome Collection, 'A noble boke off cookry ffor a prynce houssolde ...', source identifier b21529565, work identifier jx8fr4px.
evidence.locator: bibliographic metadata, digital location, provider, download, and rights
evidence.summary: The institutional record identifies the complete 1882 book, its xiii plus 136 pages, Leeds University Library as provider, a complete 164-image online copy, a public PDF, and Public Domain Mark status.

evidence.citation: Wellcome Collection IIIF Presentation 3 manifest for b21529565.
evidence.locator: manifest metadata, rights, providers, 164 canvases, rendering links, and canvases 21-143
evidence.summary: The complete IIIF manifest records the Public Domain Mark, Wellcome and Leeds providers, 164 physical canvases, image services, and complete PDF and OCR renderings.

evidence.citation: Direct visual inspection of the Wellcome digital facsimile of Napier 1882.
evidence.locator: IIIF canvases 21 and 143; printed pages 1 and 123
evidence.summary: Canvas 21 opens the medieval transcription on printed page 1; canvas 143 ends printed page 123 with the final tench recipe and manuscript trailer. The full digital object retains all surrounding edition matter.

evidence.citation: The Fitzwilliam Museum, Feast & Fast exhibition label, 'Cookery'.
evidence.summary: The label identifies an anonymous English manuscript of c. 1480-1500, written in Middle English, kept at Holkham Hall as MS 674, opening with feast menus and followed by a large recipe collection; it credits the Earl of Leicester and Trustees of the Holkham Estate.

evidence.citation: R. N. [Robina Napier], 'An Early Cookery Book,' The Antiquary 4 (September 1881): 96-98.
evidence.locator: printed pp. 96-98; PDF pages 8-10; viewer leaves n7-n9
evidence.summary: Napier describes the small rebound manuscript as about eighty leaves, written in black with red headings and ornament, partly damp-damaged but in good condition, with feast menus on the first eleven leaves.

evidence.citation: Holkham, 'The Hall' and 'Contact us', Library and Manuscripts information.
evidence.locator: Library and Archives research, image requests, linked policy documents, and Library and Manuscripts contacts
evidence.summary: Holkham welcomes scholars and researchers by arrangement, directs image requests through its collection team and Bridgeman where applicable, links current access and image-fee PDFs, and lists library@holkham.co.uk for Library and Manuscripts enquiries.

evidence.citation: Holkham, 'Access to the Collections at Holkham for Academic Study', amended 2023.
evidence.summary: The policy describes advance enquiry and appointment arrangements, research access, conditions of consultation, and archive-study fees; applicability of the fee schedule to MS 674 requires confirmation.

evidence.locator: complete fee and image-use policy PDF
evidence.summary: The linked schedule gives tiered low-resolution and full-digitization rates, a transfer/administration charge for Cambridge digitization, preservation and service caveats, and terms limiting supplied images to noncommercial research or private study unless formal reproduction or publication permission is obtained.

evidence.citation: Date-bounded exact-title and exact-shelfmark search for public images of Holkham Hall MS 674.
evidence.locator: searches for 'Holkham MS 674', 'Holkham manuscript 674', and the title; institutional and aggregator indexes
evidence.summary: No public whole-codex facsimile or selected manuscript leaves were found in Holkham's public pages, the Fitzwilliam label, Cambridge Digital Library, Scriptorium, Biblissima, Bridgeman Images, or Digital Bodleian as of the date checked.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, MARCO collection record, 'Microfilms of Holkham Manuscripts'.
evidence.locator: collection description and 103 child records; exact search for Holkham manuscript 674
evidence.summary: The catalog describes 103 Holkham microfilm records. No record for Holkham Hall MS 674 was identified; the child shelfmark MS. Film 674 reproduces other Holkham shelfmarks and is not a surrogate for Holkham MS 674.
